issues_filed.summary: Part 1 prose hardcodes acknowledged-failures count as 8 in the formula '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%'. Today uncounted-failures.json grew from 8 to 9 entries (FAIL-009 added 2026-04-20T07:00:00Z), and the og:description in docs/index.html was correspondingly updated to '9 acknowledged failures the model doesn't count'. The prose formula now drifts from both the data and the OG tag. This is also a direct CLAUDE.md violation (“All numerical counts in the HTML prose are computed from wins.json at build time. Never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data”) — same pattern as ISS-1076 (WIN-032 stale hardcoded counts, fixed 2026-04-17). Suggested fix: swap the hardcoded '8' and recomputed '85.2%' for build-time template variables (e.g. {{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}} and {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}}) or a version-neutral phrasing like “69 divided by 69 + 4 (dome-acknowledged) + N (this review’s additional uncounted failures)”. Located in data/sections.json section 2b.1 (the ‘accuracy figure is not computed’ paragraph that renders at docs/index.html line ~990).

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools / IndexNow setup (~15 min)
human_decisions_needed.context: Still PENDING (per auto-detection). No BingSiteAuth.xml, no msvalidate meta tag, no IndexNow key file in docs/. Low priority today. Counterargument: Google is ~90% US share and GSC is verified (Day 2 of the canonical 2026-04-19 to 2026-04-26 window) so today is inside the expected Google first-index window. Bing's marginal value is ~5-8% ceiling. But if Google crawl-to-index latency runs long on GitHub Pages (not uncommon for new github.io subdomains), IndexNow's push signal could materially improve time-to-first-index on Bing+Yandex. Recommendation: keep at low priority until 2026-04-23; if Google still zero by then, elevate to 'schedule this week'.

issues_for_decider.issue: Hardcoded '8' in '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%' no longer matches uncounted-failures.json entries.length (now 9 after FAIL-009 added 2026-04-20T07:00:00Z) and drifts from the just-updated og:description ('9 acknowledged failures'). CLAUDE.md forbids hardcoded numbers derivable from data — same pattern as ISS-1076 (fixed 2026-04-17).
issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: Replace hardcoded '8' and '85.2%' with build-time template tokens driven by uncounted-failures.json (e.g., {{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}} and {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}} computed as 69 / (69 + 4 + len(uncounted_failures.entries)) * 100, rounded to 1 decimal). Or: version-neutral phrasing ‘N uncounted failures this review documents’ with N filled at build time.
